source.title: Therapeutic enzyme engineering using a generative neural network

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: The paper aims to enhance the potency of mRNA therapeutics for rare diseases by engineering the therapeutic enzyme ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC) to have improved expression, half-life, and catalytic efficiency. The vast sequence space and limitations of existing methods (inaccurate or labor-intensive) motivate a novel approach combining deep latent variable modeling of sequence co-evolution with automated protein library design. The goal is to identify OTC variants that are both more thermally stable and more catalytically active, while minimizing mutations to reduce immunogenicity.

section_gists.heading: Therapeutic enzyme engineering using a generative neural network
section_gists.gist: Introduces the problem of engineering therapeutic enzymes for mRNA therapeutics, focusing on OTC. Highlights the challenge of vast sequence space and the need for methods that capture epistatic interactions. Proposes using variational autoencoders (VAEs) trained on OTC homolog sequences to generate near-human variants with improved stability and activity. Describes the training data (3818 OTC homologs) and VAE architecture, showing that the model captures site-wise and pairwise conservation statistics well (Pearson's R^2 of 0.99).
process_signals: Hypothesis: Capturing higher-order co-evolutionary relationships with a generative model will yield more functional variants than consensus approaches.
Constraint: Therapeutic proteins must function in vivo, so mutations should be minimized to reduce immunogenicity.
Decision: Use VAE to model sequence distribution and sample near-human variants.
methods: Multiple sequence alignment of OTC homologs from BLAST search, filtered to 3818 sequences.
Variational autoencoder (VAE) with convolutional encoder/decoder, latent dimension 64, trained on one-hot encoded sequences.
Weighted sampling during training to emphasize sequences similar to human OTC.
Evaluation of model fit using site-wise amino acid frequencies and pairwise mutual information.
metrics: Pearson's R^2 for pairwise mutual information: 0.99

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist: Describes the design and testing of VAE and consensus libraries. The VAE library was generated by sampling near-human variants from the trained model, while the consensus library was built using the most frequent amino acid at each position. Both libraries were screened via growth selection in an OTC-deficient E. coli strain, and ~96% of VAE variants showed improved growth. Biochemical assays revealed that the VAE library had a significantly higher mean specific activity (1.4-fold over wild-type) but similar variance compared to the consensus library. Thermal stability was improved in both libraries (8°C for consensus, 12°C for VAE), but the VAE library had a much tighter distribution of melting temperatures (variance 4.4 vs 35.5).
process_signals: Observation: High percentage of VAE variants functional in growth selection suggests screening step might be omitted in future efforts.
Comparison: VAE library outperforms consensus library in mean activity, but variances are similar.
Comparison: VAE library has higher mean thermal stability and much lower variance than consensus library.

Interpretation: Epistatic interactions captured by VAE contribute to increased catalytic rate but do not dominate; stability effects are mostly at individual residue level, but residue-residue correlations constrain extreme stability values.
methods: Library design: VAE library sampled from latent space near human OTC; consensus library constructed from most frequent amino acids.
Growth selection: OTC-deficient E. coli strain (ΔargFΔargI) grown on minimal medium; growth measured by OD600.
Biochemical assays: Specific activity measured via citrulline production; thermal stability measured by differential scanning fluorimetry (Tm).
Statistical analysis: t-test for activity differences; variance comparison.
metrics: Percentage of VAE variants with improved growth: ~96%
Mean specific activity fold change (VAE vs WT): 1.4-fold (p < 0.0001)
Variance in specific activity: consensus 0.32, VAE 0.3
failures_or_limits: Limited number of variants tested; further experiments needed to fully investigate mechanistic basis.
No trade-off observed between stability and catalysis, but reasons unclear.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Interprets the results, emphasizing that the VAE library's higher mean activity and tighter thermal stability distribution stem from capturing epistatic interactions. Argues that consensus mutations primarily affect stability at the individual residue level, while residue-residue correlations prevent extreme stability values, possibly due to evolutionary trade-offs. Notes that the lack of trade-off between stability and catalysis may be due to uncorrelated mutational effects or selection in natural sequences. Suggests future applications of VAE models for enzyme engineering and structure-function studies, and acknowledges the limitation of small library size.
process_signals: Conclusion: VAE model samples a highly functional region of sequence space, enabling efficient library design.
Hypothesis: Countervailing selective pressures encoded in residue-residue correlations constrain thermal stability distribution.
Limitation: Small number of variants tested; mechanistic basis not fully explored.
Future direction: Use VAE for detailed epistasis mapping and multiple rounds of sampling.
failures_or_limits: Limited number of variants tested; further experiments and analysis needed.
No trade-off between stability and catalysis observed, but underlying reasons not determined.

section_gists.heading: Methods
section_gists.gist: Details the computational and experimental procedures. Training data generated by BLASTp search and filtered to remove sequences with excessive gaps or short length. VAE architecture: convolutional encoder/decoder with batch normalization, ELU activations, max pooling/upsampling, latent dimension 64. Training used weighted sampling, β-annealing, and Adam optimizer. Variants sampled by encoding human OTC and decoding with noise. Consensus library built from most frequent amino acids. Experimental methods include growth selection in OTC-deficient E. coli, specific activity assay, thermal stability assay, and statistical analysis.
process_signals: Design choice: Filter sequences to remove structurally dissimilar homologs based on gappiness score.
Design choice: Weighted sampling during training to emphasize sequences similar to human OTC.
Design choice: β-annealing to balance reconstruction and KL divergence during VAE training.
Constraint: Library size limited to ~10^2 variants for experimental testing.
methods: BLASTp search with human OTC query, MAFFT alignment, gappiness filtering.
VAE with convolutional layers, latent dim 64, trained with weighted sampling and β-annealing.
Variant sampling: encode human OTC, add noise to latent vector, decode.
Consensus library: most frequent amino acid at each position.
Growth selection: ΔargFΔargI E. coli in M9 minimal medium with IPTG induction.
Thermal stability: differential scanning fluorimetry with SYPRO Orange.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The authors hypothesized that a generative model capturing higher-order co-evolutionary relationships would outperform consensus-based design in engineering OTC variants with improved stability and activity.

They expected that the VAE library would yield variants with both higher mean activity and a different distribution of thermal stabilities compared to the consensus library, due to epistatic interactions.

The results supported the hypothesis for mean activity and thermal stability distribution, but the similar variance in activity was unexpected and led to refined interpretation about the role of epistasis.
uncertainties_to_resolve: The mechanistic basis for the observed improvements in activity and stability is not fully understood; further structural and functional studies are needed.
The lack of trade-off between stability and catalysis remains unexplained; it may be due to uncorrelated mutational effects or selection in natural sequences.
The small library size limits generalizability; larger-scale studies could validate the approach and explore sequence space more thoroughly.
The paper does not describe iterative rounds of design or failures; it is unclear if initial VAE samples required refinement or if the first library was successful.

events.summary: The paper identifies the challenge of engineering therapeutic enzymes for mRNA therapeutics, noting that sequence space is vast and mapping methods are inaccurate or labor-intensive.
The authors hypothesize that capturing higher-order co-evolutionary relationships with a generative model will yield more functional variants than consensus approaches.
The study uses a variational autoencoder (VAE) trained on a large alignment of OTC sequences to model sequence co-evolution and sample novel near-human variants.
The VAE model is trained to reproduce input sequences, with an encoder mapping sequences to a latent Gaussian distribution and a decoder reconstructing them.
The model's fit is validated by comparing site-wise amino acid frequencies and pairwise mutual information between training data and generated samples, achieving high correlation.
numbers_or_metrics: Pearson's R^2 of 0.99 for site-wise frequencies
Pearson's R^2 of 0.99 for pairwise mutual information

events.summary: Analysis shows a positive correlation between Hamming distance and latent space distance, especially for sequences similar to human OTC when using weighted training.
numbers_or_metrics: Pearson's R^2 of 0.81 for top 5% similar sequences in weighted model

chunk_events.notes: This chunk introduces the problem, hypothesis, design of the VAE model, and initial validation of its ability to capture sequence statistics. It sets the stage for the experimental results.

events.summary: VAE model fit assessed for site-wise and pairwise conservation statistics, showing high correlation.
Class imbalance in training data: microbial sequences dominate, leading to prokaryotic-like samples.
Addressed class imbalance by training a weighted VAE favoring human-like sequences and sampling near hOTC.
Generated 87 near-hOTC variants by sampling from hOTC's encoded distribution with scaled variance.
Built a consensus library as an engineering control with 18 amino acid differences from hOTC.
Screened VAE and consensus variants for growth rescue of E. coli arginine auxotroph; ~96% of variants outperformed hOTC.
Growth rate of auxotroph is controlled by multiple parameters including expression, stability, and activity.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers VAE model evaluation, class imbalance handling, variant generation, consensus library design, and initial growth screening.

events.summary: All OTC variants from VAE and consensus libraries were recombinantly produced and characterized for activity and thermostability.
VAE library had a significantly higher mean specific activity (1.4-fold over hOTC) compared to consensus library (0.96-fold).
All VAE variants exhibited improved thermostability, with average Tm 73°C (12°C improvement over hOTC), while consensus library had average Tm 68°C (8°C improvement) but wider spread.
VAE method boosts hit rate in upper right quadrant (improved thermostability and specific activity) compared to consensus.
Top 12 VAE variants with highest specific activity were synthesized as codon-optimized mRNAs for in vitro evaluation in mammalian cells.
mRNAs were transfected into HepG2 cells, and OTC protein expression and activity were measured.
Despite lower expression levels, seven variants showed same or slightly improved total activity, and all variants had 1–1.5-fold greater specific activity than hOTC.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains experimental characterization of VAE and consensus libraries, comparison of activity and stability, and validation in mammalian cells.

events.summary: Used regularized least squares linear regression to estimate effects of individual mutations on specific activity and thermostability.
Identified specific mutations that mutually increase or decrease activity and stability, or affect one property while neutral to the other.
Observed that specific activity and melting temperature do not appear to be globally anti-correlated, contrary to stability-activity trade-off assumption.
Mapped important mutations onto OTC crystal structure to rationalize effects on stability and activity.
Interpreted that VAE library's higher mean activity suggests epistatic interactions contribute to increased catalytic rate, but comparable variance indicates epistasis does not dominate.
Interpreted that consensus mutations primarily affect stability at individual residue level, while residue-residue correlations constrain extreme stability values.
Acknowledged limited number of variants tested and need for further experiments to fully investigate mechanistic basis.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains detailed analysis of mutation effects using regression, structural mapping, and interpretation of library differences, directly supporting the paper's research evolution.

events.summary: Designed VAE architecture with convolutional encoder/decoder, latent dimension 64, and weighted sampling to emphasize sequences similar to human OTC.
Sampled near-human OTC variants from VAE by encoding human OTC, adding noise to latent vector, and decoding, limiting mutations to ~5%.
Assessed VAE model's ability to capture first-order conservation and second-order correlations using entropy and mutual information.
Performed growth assays in OTC-deficient E. coli auxotroph to test functionality of VAE and consensus variants.
Tested activity of OTC variants in HepG2 cells by transient transfection of mRNA and measuring enzyme activity normalized to expression.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains detailed methods for data generation, VAE design, variant sampling, consensus library construction, and experimental assays. It provides explicit design choices and experimental procedures, which are core to the paper's process.

events.summary: Linear regression analysis was applied to estimate contributions of individual substitutions to specific activity and melting temperature.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk primarily describes the linear regression and structural analysis methods used to analyze the experimental data. It does not introduce new hypotheses or major findings, but provides methodological details for the analysis phase.

evidence_bank.quote: sequence space is incomprehensibly vast, and methods to map sequence to function (computationally or experimentally) are inaccurate or time-/labor-intensive.

the most effective sequence design methods may need to consider not just position- or pair-wise conservation when making mutations, but also the interactions between all positions in a given sequence alignment.
we applied variational autoencoders (VAEs), a recent advance from the deep learning literature
we built a large multi-sequence alignment of OTC homologs obtained by BLAST search. After filtering these sequences to remove those that caused the most gaps, we were left with 3818 sequences
The encoder maps each sequence in the training dataset to a small set of latent factors that represent the parameters of a multi-dimensional Gaussian distribution.
The training objective for the networks is to reproduce the original sequences from the decoded samples.
Per position amino acid frequency in VAE samples match those in the training data.
scatter plot of all amino acid frequencies (training vs. samples), (Pearson's $R^{2}$ value of 0.99).
Second-order correlations in VAE samples match those in the training data.
a scatter plot of position-to-position mutual information between the training data and the samples (Pearson's $R^{2}$ value of 0.99).
Positive correlation between Hamming and latent space distance.
In the weighted model, the 5% most similar training data exhibit higher correlation than the training data as a whole (Pearson's $R^{2}$ values of 0.81 vs 0.56, dashed lines).
Alignment averages of the training data and random samples from the model matched exceptionally well (Fig. 1C, Pearson's $R^{2}$ of 0.99)
A linear fit to all pairwise MI values shows strong correlations (Fig. 1D, right, Pearson's $R^{2}$ of 0.99)
the strong bias for microbial genomes in sequence databases results in a type of class imbalance, where the number of eukaryotic (and specifically mammalian) OTCs are in the minority
random sampling from an unweighted model is likely to result in prokaryotic-like sequences that might be less suitable for therapeutic applications

we trained both an unweighted VAE and a VAE where the training data was sampled to favor human-like sequences (the weights are inversely proportional to the Hamming distance between each sequence and hOTC)
we decided to encode hOTC and sample repeatedly from its encoded distribution (Fig. 2A)
This method yielded 87 unique near-hOTC variants (with an average of $>98\%$ and no less than 95% identity) with mutations spanning the length of the gene at 32 distinct locations (Fig. 2B)
The variants on average contained 8 amino acid substitutions relative to hOTC (Fig. 2C)
we identified 18 amino acid differences between the hOTC wildtype sequence and the consensus
We encoded these amino acid substitutions into a fully combinatorial library of $\sim10^{7}$ variants and randomly selected 91 unique variants
almost all VAE (84/87, orange) and consensus (87/91, blue) variants outperformed hOTC (green), promoting OD600 increases of 1.5–2.5-fold
the growth rate of the E. coli auxotroph is controlled by multiple parameters, including protein expression (from improved translation or stability) and enzymatic activity
all OTC variants (i.e., both VAE and consensus variants) were recombinantly produced, and activity and thermostability were characterized

comparison of the mean activity between the two libraries (solid black lines) indicates a statistically significant difference (p value <0.0001); the consensus library on average has a mean activity that is 0.96-fold relative to hOTC while the VAE library shows a significant improvement with a mean activity of 1.4-fold relative to hOTC.
all VAE variants exhibited improved thermostability with respect to hOTC

the most striking difference between the two methods is seen when comparing the two quadrants in the upper half of the panel. The consensus approach yields more variants in the upper left quadrant (improved thermostability, but decreased specific activity), while the VAE method boosts the hit rate in the upper right quadrant (improved thermostability and improved specific activity).
the 12 VAE sequences with highest specific activity were synthesized as codon-optimized mRNAs for in vitro evaluation.
The 12 VAE OTC mRNAs were synthesized as described in Materials and Methods and transfected into HepG2 cells
OTC protein expression and activity were measured in total lysates one day after transfection.
Expression levels of the variants were on par or lower (0.5–0.75-fold) than that observed with hOTC
seven of the variants showed the same or slightly improved total activity in cell lysates relative to hOTC
the specific activities of all OTC variants were 1–1.5-fold greater than that of hOTC
four variants showing a statistically significant improvement in specific activity
We used regularized least squares linear regression to estimate the effects of individual mutations on specific activity and thermostability.
Several single mutations either mutually increase specific activity and melting temperature (e.g., A217I, A217V, S60A) or decrease both properties (e.g., A217T, and K70 and K353 deletions).

Taken as a whole, however, specific activity and melting temperature do not appear to be globally anti-correlated, as might have been expected if the sequences were close to optimal with respect to both high specific activity and high melting temperature.
Several mutations of interest are highlighted on the OTC crystal structure in Fig. 7.
The enhanced mean activity observed for the VAE library suggests that the epistatic interactions captured in this library contribute to increased catalytic rate.

However, the observation that the VAE and consensus libraries exhibit comparable variance in activity suggests that the residues involved in epistatic interactions do not dominate in their contributions to catalytic activity.

The similarity in thermal stability between the libraries may reflect the fact that the VAE library is highly enriched in consensus mutations and suggests that the effects on stability are mostly accounted for at the individual residue level, rather than epistasis between residues.
In contrast, the difference in the spread of thermal stability in the OTC libraries is consistent with two countervailing selective pressures operating at the level of residue-residue correlations.

However, we were limited in the number of variants we tested, and further experiments and analysis will be needed to fully investigate the physical/mechanistic basis of library mutations on OTC stability and catalytic activity.
Weighted sampling with replacement was used to preferentially select training data for each batch based on the similarity to hOTC.
Our VAE consists of two convolutional neural networks (an encoder and a decoder).
We used a latent space of dimension 64.
To sample near-human variants from the VAE for testing, we first encoded the hOTC sequence, resulting in a set of means and variances.
After removing duplicates, this resulted in the 87 variants tested in this work.

To assess the ability of our VAE model to capture first-order sequence conservation and second-order sequence correlations in the evolutionary dataset, we use two metrics from information theory: entropy and mutual information.

Enzyme activity (expressed as nmol citrulline/min/mg total protein) was normalized to relative OTC expression as judged by densitometry analysis of the western blot.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Engineering therapeutic enzymes for mRNA therapeutics is challenging due to vast sequence space and inaccurate or labor-intensive mapping methods. The goal is to improve expression, half-life, and catalytic efficiency of ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC) while minimizing mutations to reduce immunogenicity.

canonical_events.summary: Identify challenge: vast sequence space and inaccurate/labor-intensive mapping methods hinder therapeutic enzyme engineering for mRNA therapeutics.
Hypothesize that capturing higher-order co-evolutionary relationships with a generative model (VAE) will yield more functional variants than consensus approaches.
Design VAE model trained on 3818 OTC homolog sequences to model sequence co-evolution and sample near-human variants.
Address class imbalance by training weighted VAE favoring human-like sequences and sampling near hOTC.
Validate VAE model fit: site-wise amino acid frequencies and pairwise mutual information match training data with Pearson's R^2 of 0.99.
Generate VAE library: sample 87 near-hOTC variants with average >98% identity, 8 substitutions on average.
Build consensus library as control: 18 amino acid differences from hOTC, ~10^7 variants, randomly select 91 unique variants.
Screen VAE and consensus variants for growth rescue of E. coli arginine auxotroph; ~96% of variants outperform hOTC.
Characterize all OTC variants for specific activity and thermal stability; VAE library shows 1.4-fold higher mean activity (p<0.0001) and 12°C Tm improvement vs 8°C for consensus.
VAE library boosts hit rate in upper right quadrant (improved thermostability and specific activity) compared to consensus.
Test top 12 VAE variants as codon-optimized mRNAs in HepG2 cells; despite lower expression, specific activity is 1–1.5-fold greater than hOTC.
Linear regression identifies mutations affecting activity and stability; no global anti-correlation between specific activity and melting temperature.

Interpret that VAE library's higher mean activity suggests epistatic interactions contribute to catalytic rate, but comparable variance indicates epistasis does not dominate; consensus mutations primarily affect stability at individual residue level, while residue-residue correlations constrain extreme stability values.
Acknowledge limited number of variants tested; further experiments needed to fully investigate mechanistic basis.

edges.reason: Class imbalance issue refines training approach.
Weighted VAE training enables model validation.
Validated model used to generate VAE library.
Consensus library built as control after VAE library generation.
Consensus library also screened for comparison.
Growth screening leads to biochemical characterization.
Activity and stability data enable quadrant analysis.
Top variants from characterization tested in mammalian cells.
Regression results inform interpretation of epistasis and stability.

must_cover_points: Problem of vast sequence space and need for better methods.
Hypothesis that VAE captures epistasis better than consensus.
VAE design and training with weighted sampling.
Model validation showing high correlation for site-wise and pairwise statistics.
Generation of VAE library (87 near-human variants).
Biochemical characterization: VAE library has higher mean activity and tighter thermal stability distribution.
Mammalian cell testing confirming improved specific activity.
Mutation effect analysis and lack of stability-activity trade-off.
Acknowledged limitation of small library size.
